projectDescription: A binary image classification project that uses a ResNet-based deep learning model (via ImageAI) to detect whether Kermit the Frog from The Muppets TV show appears in a given image or video frame.
keyFeatures: Binary image classification: kermit vs. no-kermit
Prediction on both static images and video files (frame-by-frame at 1-second intervals)
Asynchronous batch prediction for video frames
Dataset enrichment via image rotation (90°, -90°, 180°) and Google Images download
Trained model stored in HDF5 format (kermit_finder.h5) with a JSON class map
publicApiSurface: imageai_build_model.py — trains a CustomImagePrediction ResNet model
kermit_model_evaluation.py -t image -f <file> — predicts one or more images
kermit_model_evaluation.py -t video -f <file> — predicts all frames of a video
helpers/convert_vid2image.py — extracts frames from a video file
helpers/rotate_images.py — augments training images with rotations
helpers/download_from_google.py — downloads additional Kermit images from Google
helpers/utils.py::gather_dict(tasks) — async helper to await a dict of coroutines
data/images/json/model_class.json — class index mapping {0: kermit, 1: no-kermit}
